notes:
Works with the fixes mentioned below (renaming the exe + config files)
BUT if you want to play with two controllers (local multiplayer), we had to select the "reboot now" option when prompted. After the game restarts, two-player will work.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: The Launcher and Game EXE need swapped around, so rename Launcher.exe to anything, and sonic2app.exe to Launcher.exe. This makes Steam think you're launching the game. Also, for controller input to work, you need to copy some configuration files into the game folder as others have mentioned. Outside of this, the game runs flawlessly with no crashes or slowdowns.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: I tried vanilla and also followed the directions (copied the savegames and renamed the exe). I tried several Proton versions. None of them even started the game.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: Moved the config files and renamed the launcher.exe as stated below. Also tried disabling Esync and D3D11, neither works. The game prepares to launch, Steam says "Running..." for a few seconds, and then it stops.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: -Rename sonic2app.exe to Launcher.exe, put the config files under Configs, and edit the resolution in UserConfig.cfg to be your resolution (mine was 1360x786, and it wouldn't work until I changed this). Runs beautifully now.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: After renaming the Sonic.exe to Launcher.exe and manually create the UserConig.cfg and Keyboard.cfg file inside the config folder, the game runs perfectly with my xbox 360 controller. also the button mapping / ingame buttons are correct. just google for "sonic adventure 2 userconfig.cfg" and you will find a steam community thread. you will find an example, how the cfg files must look like, to let the game start.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: Runs just fine after renaming the launcher as listed below and using this guide to replace the configs: https://steamcommunity.com/app/213610/discussions/0/846938350589831838/
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: Performance is perfect, basically native. But getting the game to run requires some effort. Rename "Launcher.exe" to anything else, and rename "sonic2app.exe" to "Launcher.exe". This is because, like other games such as Borderlands GOTY Enhanced, the launcher doesn't work with Proton (probably a .NET incompatibility or something). As a result of the launcher not running, you'll need to import configurations to get controls working. Other people have linked to this. Once you've followed these steps, the game will work perfectly.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

title: Sonic Adventure 2

cpu: AMD A10-8700P Radeon R6, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes:
This game will not run out the box so you have to do some tweaks first to get it to run.

1. Go to the install directory for SA2 and rename Launcher.exe to sonic2app.exe, and sonic2app.exe to Launcher.exe

2. Go to Configs directory and create 2 new empty files here. Keyboard.cfg and UserConfig.cfg

3. Go to this link and copy each of those configs and save them to those 2 files you created as outlined https://pcgamingwiki.com/wiki/Sonic_Adventure_2#Launcher_crashes_when_trying_to_start_the_game.2C_No_resolution_options_available

4. Be sure to change the resolution in your UserConfig.cfg to match your display or whatever you want it set at.

Extra Notes: The Framerate default is 60 FPS which is also tied to the game speed at which it will be running at. This is an undesirable result however making it slightly difficult or harder to play the game at a normal speed rate, and cutscenes won't play at normal speed either.
title: Sonic Adventure 2

notes: Must rename "Launcher.exe" to anything else, and "sonic2app.exe" to "Launcher.exe," as due to issues present in standard WINE as well as Proton, the game's launcher doesn't work. You also have to manually import game config files which are meant to be generated by the launcher, but of course are not because "Launcher.exe" does not run. After this, the game will work like native! Played about 20 hours with Proton, tested out cutscenes, all levels, Chao Garden, Sound Test (clearly the best part of the game) and encountered no issues. Played with the Steam Controller.
title: Sonic Adventure 2
